name: Tavernkeeper Knowledge
description: SillyTavern internals knowledge bank, bundled with Tavernkeeper's Workshop and served on demand through the workshop_search_knowledge tool.

changelog.summary: Knowledge is now served by the workshop_search_knowledge tool instead of a synced lorebook; read tools cover characters, Quick Reply sets, and regex scripts; untagged-JSON detection was removed.
Managed extension tools can now create, inspect, atomically update, and roll back small UI extensions through the optional Tavernkeeper Writer server companion.

0.content: SillyTavern architecture: a Node.js Express server (src/, REST endpoints under /api/) serving a no-build jQuery + ES-modules SPA (public/). public/script.js is the core (chat state, generation, eventSource); feature modules live in public/scripts/*.js; SillyTavern.getContext() (public/scripts/st-context.js) is the stable API surface for scripts and extensions. All user data lives in data/<user-handle>/ (default handle: default-user): characters/, chats/, worlds/, QuickReplies/, extensions/ (third-party installs), themes/, settings.json, secrets.json. Server config is config.yaml at the repo root. Universal rules: mutate live state, then call the matching debounced saver — saveSettingsDebounced() for extension_settings, saveMetadataDebounced() for chat_metadata, saveChatConditional() for the chat array; a missing saver call is the top cause of changes vanishing on reload. Prefer SillyTavern.getContext() over deep relative imports. jQuery, toastr, DOMPurify, moment are globals. Mobile (~360px, touch) is a first-class target.

1.content: Character Card V2: { "spec": "chara_card_v2", "spec_version": "2.0", "data": {...} } — everything lives under data: name, description, personality, scenario, first_mes, mes_example, creator_notes, system_prompt, post_history_instructions, alternate_greetings[], tags[], creator, character_version, extensions{}, character_book. Always write \{\{char\}\}/\{\{user\}\}, never literal names, so cards survive renames and persona switches. mes_example: dialogue blocks separated by <START> lines with \{\{user\}\}:/\{\{char\}\}: speakers; pushed out of context first when space runs low. system_prompt and post_history_instructions OVERRIDE the user's own preset unless they contain \{\{original\}\} (which splices the user's value in) — leave them empty unless truly needed. creator_notes is the only field never sent to the model. first_mes + alternate_greetings are the swipeable greetings and set writing style more than any other field. Token budget: description + personality + scenario ≈ permanent context; 300-800 tokens is the sweet spot; plain prose beats W++/JSON trait lists on modern models. V3 (chara_card_v3, spec_version 3.0) adds assets, nickname, group_only_greetings, creator_notes_multilingual, creation_date — produce V2 unless V3 features are needed; everything accepts V2.

2.content: The card's data.extensions block (preserved on export by spec): talkativeness ("0.0"-"1.0", group-chat speaking frequency), fav, world (name of a lorebook FILE to link — it does not embed the book; embedding is character_book), depth_prompt { prompt, depth, role: system|user|assistant } — a character-bound Author's-Note-style injection at depth N; the classic character-stability trick. regex_scripts: array of RegexScriptData { id (UUID), scriptName, findRegex, replaceString, trimStrings[], placement[] (which text it runs on — user input, AI output, slash commands, World Info, reasoning; numeric enum in public/scripts/extensions/regex/engine.js), disabled, markdownOnly, promptOnly, runOnEdit, substituteRegex, minDepth, maxDepth } — users must approve embedded regex scripts on import. Extensions store custom card data via writeExtensionField(characterId, key, value) from getContext(); it lands under data.extensions[key] and survives export/import.

3.comment: Embedded character_book format vs native world file
3.content: Embedded V2 books use a DIFFERENT shape than native world files. In character_book, entries is an ARRAY of { id, keys[], secondary_keys[], comment, content, constant, selective, insertion_order, enabled, position ('before_char'|'after_char'), extensions{} }. ST converts on import via convertCharacterBook (public/scripts/world-info.js): keys→key, secondary_keys→keysecondary, insertion_order→order, enabled→!disable. ST-native extras go inside each entry's extensions{} with snake_case names: position (numeric override), depth, probability, prevent_recursion, exclude_recursion, delay_until_recursion, group, group_weight, group_override, use_group_scoring, sticky, cooldown, delay, automation_id, role, scan_depth, case_sensitive, match_whole_words, vectorized, triggers, ignore_budget. Native standalone files (data/<handle>/worlds/*.json) instead use entries as an OBJECT keyed by uid-as-string with native field names. CRITICAL: an embedded character_book is INERT at generation time — ST only scans standalone world files, so the embedded book does nothing until the user imports it via 'Import Card Lore'. The card's extensions.world field links a standalone world FILE by name and silently no-ops if that file does not exist. Embedded books travel with the card (character-private lore); standalone books are shareable across characters.

4.content: Native world file: data/<handle>/worlds/<Name>.json, entries as an object keyed by uid-as-string, uid matching its key (0..n). Core fields: key[] — plain strings match whole-word, case-insensitive by default; '/regex/flags' strings are supported and are the right tool for stems and variants. keysecondary[] + selectiveLogic — second condition vs the same scan text: 0 = AND ANY, 1 = NOT ALL, 2 = NOT ANY, 3 = AND ALL. constant: true — always inserted, no keys needed (budget still applies unless ignoreBudget). comment — the entry title in the UI; always fill it. order — sort within the same position; HIGHER = closer to the end = more influence. position — 0 before char defs, 1 after char defs, 2/3 top/bottom of Author's Note, 4 at `depth` in-chat as `role` (0 system, 1 user, 2 assistant) — the strongest steering position with small depth, 5/6 before/after example messages, 7 named outlet: set outletName on the entry and render it anywhere macros expand with \{\{outlet::name\}\} (presets, Author's Note, depth prompts). probability — % chance per activation. disable, vectorized (only activated by Vector Storage similarity search). scanDepth / caseSensitive / matchWholeWords — per-entry overrides; null = inherit global. Additional match sources (per-entry booleans, checked besides recent chat): matchPersonaDescription, matchCharacterDescription, matchCharacterPersonality, matchCharacterDepthPrompt, matchScenario, matchCreatorNotes. triggers[] restricts activation to generation types (normal, continue, impersonate, swipe, regenerate, quiet; empty = all). Books bind globally (Active Worlds), to a character (card extensions.world or auxiliary charLore), to a chat, or to a persona — all bound books are scanned together every generation.

5.content: WI activation, every generation: the last scanDepth messages are joined and scanned (plus any enabled extra match sources like persona/character description) → matched + constant entries collect → probability rolls → inclusion-group filtering → sort by order → inserted until the Context %/token budget cap — highest-order entries that overflow are silently DROPPED (the browser console 'World Info' log lists activated and skipped entries; check it whenever entries mysteriously vanish) → recursion passes rescan inserted content up to the global max. Entry never fires, in order of likelihood: key mismatch (whole-word matching is on by default — 'Eldor' will not match 'Eldoria'), scan depth shallower than the keyword's last mention, budget exhausted, entry disabled, or the wrong book bound. Fires but has no effect: position too weak — move to @ Depth (position 4) with depth 2-4 and raise order. Global settings that change behavior: Scan Depth, Context %, Recursive scan, Match whole words, Include names (group chats), Min activations + depth cap.

6.content: WI timed effects: sticky (entry stays active N messages after triggering — 3-5 for scene lore so it doesn't flicker), cooldown (cannot retrigger for N messages — one-shot flavor events), delay (inactive until the chat has ≥ N messages). Recursion: inserted entry content is itself scanned, so entries can trigger other entries. excludeRecursion = other entries cannot match against my content; preventRecursion = my content triggers nothing; delayUntilRecursion = I only activate during recursion passes (an integer ≥ 1 sets the recursion level — staged reveals and secrets). Inclusion groups: group is a comma-separated label list; among simultaneously-triggered entries sharing a label only one wins — weighted by groupWeight, or scored by key-match count with useGroupScoring; groupOverride forces this entry. Use for mutually exclusive lore variants. Decorators on the first lines of content: @@activate / @@dont_activate force or suppress the entry. automationId fires the Quick Reply with the same Automation ID when the entry activates — the WI→script bridge. probability 20-40% works for ambient color.

7.content: Lorebook craft: write entry content as the model should see it — declarative prose, third person, present tense; never 'this entry describes'. Macros expand inside content (\{\{char\}\}, \{\{user\}\}, \{\{getvar::x\}\} for dynamic lore). One concept per entry, 50-300 tokens; giant entries blow the budget and starve everything else. Key discipline: include the concept's name, aliases, nicknames, and the misspellings players actually type; one regex key like /kings?|royal(ty)?/i beats ten near-duplicate string keys. Layer the book: 1-3 tiny constant entries for world pillars + keyed entries for everything else + delayUntilRecursion entries for secrets that surface when related lore appears. Set preventRecursion on narrative-voice entries that mention many keywords, or they cascade. For state machines (quests, arcs): pair variables with WI — a constant entry rendering \{\{getvar::arc\}\} into the prompt, entries keyed to phrases a script injects, or automationId firing Quick Replies on activation. Character-private lore belongs in the embedded character_book; shared setting lore in a standalone world file.

8.content: STscript is a |-piped chain of slash commands, runnable from the chat input, Quick Replies, and code via executeSlashCommandsWithOptions(). Each command's return feeds the next; read it with \{\{pipe\}\}. Named args (key=value) come before the unnamed argument; quote values with spaces (key="two words"). Closures {: ... :} are first-class sub-scripts used for branches, loop bodies, and subroutines: /let doubler {: n= /mul \{\{var::n\}\} 2 :} | /:doubler n=4 — /:name calls a closure, and closure named args become scoped variables. Scoped (script-local) vars: /let x 5, /var key=x 6 to reassign, \{\{var::x\}\} to read — prefer them inside scripts (no persistence, no collisions). Flow control: /if left=x rule=eq right=1 {: then :} else={: else :} with rules eq neq lt gt lte gte in nin not (in = substring/list membership); /while left=.. rule=.. right=.. guard=on {: body :} (guard=on caps at 100 iterations — keep it on); /times 5 {: /echo \{\{timesIndex\}\} :}; /break exits loops/closures, /abort stops the whole script, /return returns from a closure. Comments: /# text |. Escaping: \| for a literal pipe, \{\{...\}\} to prevent macro expansion. Multi-line scripts: write them in the Quick Reply editor (newlines + highlighting; | at line end continues the chain).

9.content: Commands worth reaching for: /echo (toast notification), /send (add user message), /sendas name=X text (add message as anyone), /sys (narrator message), /comment (visible note the LLM never sees), /hide range (exclude messages from prompt), /trigger (fire a generation), /gen and /genraw (generate to the pipe WITHOUT adding a message — background LLM calls), /input (popup prompt, returns to pipe), /buttons labels=["A","B"] text (choice popup), /delay ms, /messages, /cut, /addswipe, /delswipe, /inject id=x position=chat depth=4 text (prompt injection persisted in chat metadata; list with /listinjects, clear with /flushinject), /world name (toggle books), /getentryfield /setentryfield /createentry /findentry (edit WI from scripts), /qr-* family (manage Quick Replies), /run scriptName-or-closure, /import-script (reusable libraries), /regex-state (report a regex script's status), /reasoning-get /reasoning-set /reasoning-parse /reasoning-collapse /reasoning-expand /reasoning-toggle (work with reasoning blocks; accept ranges). Discovery: type / in the chat box for autocomplete; /help slash documents everything. To confirm a command exists in a given install, grep public/scripts for name: '<command>'.

10.content: Register commands with the object API — the old registerSlashCommand() is deprecated. Destructure SlashCommandParser, SlashCommand, SlashCommandArgument, SlashCommandNamedArgument, ARGUMENT_TYPE from SillyTavern.getContext(), then SlashCommandParser.addCommandObject(SlashCommand.fromProps({ name, aliases, returns, callback: async (namedArgs, unnamedArg) => string, namedArgumentList: [SlashCommandNamedArgument.fromProps({ name, description, typeList, isRequired, defaultValue, enumProvider })], unnamedArgumentList: [...], helpString })). The callback's return value becomes \{\{pipe\}\} — always return a string ('' if nothing). Named args arrive as strings or closures; namedArgs._scope and namedArgs._abortController are injected extras. ARGUMENT_TYPE: STRING, NUMBER, RANGE, BOOLEAN, VARIABLE_NAME, CLOSURE, SUBCOMMAND, LIST, DICTIONARY — accepting CLOSURE lets callers pass {: :} bodies you run with closure.execute(). enumProvider powers autocomplete (ready-made providers in SlashCommandCommonEnumsProvider.js). helpString is HTML shown in /help slash — it is the command's entire UX. Register once at init; duplicate names throw. Run scripts from code: const r = await executeSlashCommandsWithOptions('/getvar mood', { handleParserErrors: true }); r.pipe holds the final value, r.isError/r.errorMessage on failure.

11.content: Three variable scopes. Scoped: script-local, one run — /let, /var, \{\{var::x\}\}. Chat (local): stored in chat_metadata.variables (line 1 of the chat jsonl), lives with this chat — /setvar key=x 1, /getvar x, /addvar key=x 2, /incvar, /decvar, /flushvar, /listvar; macros \{\{getvar::x\}\}, \{\{setvar::x::v\}\}. Global: extension_settings.variables.global in settings.json, everywhere — same commands with global (/setglobalvar, /getglobalvar, ...); macros \{\{getglobalvar::x\}\}. Values are strings (coerced for arithmetic). JSON works: /setvar key=inv ["sword","rope"], /addvar appends to arrays, index= reads by array index or object key; the macro equivalents are \{\{getvarkey::inv::0\}\} and \{\{setvarkey::inv::0::axe\}\} (+global variants). From code: ctx.variables.local.set('mood','grim') (saves metadata for you), ctx.variables.global.get('x'). Chat vars die with the chat and don't follow branches created earlier; global vars are account-wide — namespace them (myext_state). Variables expand in card fields, Author's Note, WI content, and depth prompts — 'Current mood: \{\{getvar::mood\}\}' inside a constant WI entry is a cheap state channel to the model; in macros, .name is shorthand for a chat variable and $name for a global one. Auto-execute Quick Replies + variables = state machines (turn counters, HP trackers, quest stages).

12.content: Macros are handled by the Macros 2.0 engine (public/scripts/macros/, default since ST 1.16): a real parser with a registry, autocomplete with inline docs (type \{\{ in most fields), and nesting. Macros expand in card fields, WI content, Author's Note, Quick Replies, depth prompts, and slash-command arguments — NOT in plain chat messages the user types. Escape literal braces with a backslash: \{ and \}. Conditionals: \{\{if condition\}\}then\{\{else\}\}otherwise\{\{/if\}\} — the condition can be a bare macro name (auto-resolved), a nested macro like \{\{getvar::x\}\}, or a variable shorthand; prefix ! to invert; falsy = empty, 'false', 'off', '0'. Variable shorthands: .name reads a chat variable, $name a global. Scoped blocks: \{\{setvar::x\}\}long content\{\{/setvar\}\} (block content becomes the last argument; scoped content is trimmed unless the # flag is used: \{\{#setvar::x\}\}). Scoped comments \{\{//\}\}...\{\{///\}\} remove everything between them. Variable macros: getvar, setvar, addvar, incvar, decvar, hasvar, deletevar, getvarkey, setvarkey (+ global variants of all). Identity/card: char, user, persona, group, notChar, groupNotMuted, charDescription, charPersonality, charScenario, charPrompt, charInstruction, charDepthPrompt, charCreatorNotes, charFirstMessage/greeting (takes an index for alternate greetings), charVersion, mesExamples, mesExamplesRaw, model, original, input, pipe. Chat state: lastMessage, lastUserMessage, lastCharMessage, lastMessageId, firstIncludedMessageId, firstDisplayedMessageId, currentSwipeId, lastSwipeId, allChatRange, lastGenerationType. Context limits: maxPrompt, maxContext, maxResponse. Utility: outlet::key (renders a WI outlet entry anywhere), banned::word (adds to banned words on Text Completion), isMobile, hasExtension, trim, noop, newline, space, reverse, \{\{// inline comment\}\}. Time: time, date, weekday, isotime, isodate, datetimeformat DD.MM.YYYY HH:mm, timeDiff::a::b, idleDuration. Randomness: \{\{random:a,b,c\}\} (rerolls every expansion), \{\{pick:a,b,c\}\} (stable per message — survives rerolls), \{\{roll:d20\}\} (also 1d6+2 style).

13.content: Register custom macros through the Macros 2.0 registry — the old registerMacro(name, fn)/unregisterMacro on getContext() are deprecated. const { macros } = SillyTavern.getContext(); macros.register('myMacro', { category: macros.category.MISC, description: 'shown in autocomplete and the macro browser', unnamedArgs: [{ name: 'arg', description: '...', optional: true }], returns: 'what it evaluates to', exampleUsage: ['\{\{myMacro::x\}\}'], handler: ({ unnamedArgs: [arg], env, flags }) => 'result string' }). The handler must return a string synchronously — macros expand inline during prompt build, no async. Categories: utility, random, names, character, chat, time, variable, prompts, state, misc. macros.registerAlias(existing, alias) adds an alias; macros.registry.unregisterMacro(name) removes one (call it when your extension is disabled). Registered macros automatically get autocomplete with docs everywhere, appear in the macro browser, and work as bare names in \{\{if\}\} conditions. Advanced: delayArgResolution: true hands the handler raw unresolved argument text plus a resolve() callback, so you only evaluate the branch you take — this is how \{\{if\}\} avoids executing both branches.

14.content: Quick Replies are named STscript buttons organized into sets: data/<handle>/QuickReplies/<Set>.json, enabled globally (Extensions > Quick Replies) or per-chat (chat sets travel with exported chats). Set schema: { version: 2, name, disableSend (click puts text in input instead of sending), placeBeforeInput, injectInput, qrList: [{ id, showLabel, label, title (tooltip), message, isHidden, preventAutoExecute, executeOnStartup, executeOnUser, executeOnAi, executeOnChatChange, executeOnNewChat, executeOnGroupMemberDraft, automationId, contextList }], idIndex }. message is the STscript (multi-line OK); if it doesn't start with /, clicking types it into the input. isHidden = invisible but callable — put shared logic in hidden QRs and /run Set.Label from others; that is QR's module system. contextList [{ set, isChained }] makes the button open another set as a nested menu. Auto-execute flags turn QRs into event handlers without writing an extension — an executeOnAi QR that runs /incvar + /inject is a turn counter/status bar. automationId fires the QR when a World Info entry with the same Automation ID activates. Script management: /qr-create set=X label=Y "/echo hi", /qr-update, /qr-delete, /qr-arg, /run Set.Label. From code: globalThis.quickReplyApi (createSet, createQuickReply, getQrByLabel, executeQuickReply) — check it exists first; the extension can be disabled. Keep labels one-thought + emoji; phones wrap the bar.

15.content: UI extensions are plain ES-module folders — no build step, no bundled npm deps. Built-ins: public/scripts/extensions/<name>/; user-installed: data/<handle>/extensions/<repo>/ served at /scripts/extensions/third-party/<repo>/; users install from the Extensions panel with a GitHub URL, so manifest.json must sit at the repo root. manifest.json: { display_name, js, css (entry points), author, version, homePage, auto_update, loading_order (lower loads earlier), dependencies (other extension folder names), minimum_client_version, i18n, generate_interceptor, hooks: { install|update|delete|clean|enable|disable|activate: "exportedFnName" } }. Initialize on APP_READY (or the activate hook), never at import time — DOM and settings aren't ready during module evaluation. Settings pattern: extension_settings[MODULE] seeded with structuredClone(defaults), forward-fill new keys after updates, save with saveSettingsDebounced(). Templates: renderExtensionTemplateAsync('third-party/<folder>', 'settings') fetches and renders a Handlebars HTML file; inlining an HTML string is also fine. Quality bar: native ST classes and theme variables, works at 360px touch-only, listeners idempotent (no double-bind on re-init), errors surfaced with toastr.error, no console spam.

16.content: SillyTavern.getContext() is the supported API surface (full list: public/scripts/st-context.js); prefer it over deep relative imports, which break when ST moves files. State: chat (the mutable message array — messages have .mes, .is_user, .extra, .swipes), characters, characterId, groups, groupId, chatId, chatMetadata, name1/name2, extensionSettings, mainApi, maxContext. Persistence: saveSettingsDebounced(), saveMetadataDebounced(), saveChat(), writeExtensionField(characterId, key, value). Generation: generateQuietPrompt({ quietPrompt }) — background LLM call returning a string; generateRaw({ prompt }) — no chat context; Generate() — the full pipeline; stopGeneration(); setExtensionPrompt(key, value, position, depth, scan, role) — inject text into every prompt (how Author's Note and Summary work); substituteParams(text) — expand macros. UI: callGenericPopup(html, POPUP_TYPE.CONFIRM|TEXT|INPUT), the Popup class, addOneMessage(), updateMessageBlock(id, mes) — call after mutating a rendered message, messageFormatter (markdown), t (i18n tagged template), showLoader/hideLoader. Data: getTokenCountAsync(text), loadWorldInfo(name), saveWorldInfo(name, data), getWorldInfoNames() (all world file names), updateWorldInfoList(), convertCharacterBook(book) (embedded→native), getRequestHeaders() — required CSRF headers for any fetch to /api/..., getCharacterCardFields(), getExtensionManifest(folder), isMobile(). Also: eventSource + eventTypes, SlashCommandParser family, executeSlashCommandsWithOptions, macros (the Macros 2.0 registry namespace — registerMacro/unregisterMacro are deprecated), registerFunctionTool (LLM function calling), registerDebugFunction (adds a Debug-menu button). To add a message from code: push onto chat, await addOneMessage(msg), await saveChat() — or simpler, run /sendas via executeSlashCommandsWithOptions.

17.content: Where to mount UI: settings panels → #extensions_settings or #extensions_settings2, wrapped in ST's collapsible drawer skeleton (.inline-drawer > .inline-drawer-toggle.inline-drawer-header with an .inline-drawer-icon chevron + .inline-drawer-content); wand menu left of the input (per-chat actions) → #extensionsMenu with a .extensionsMenuExtensionButton item; per-message action buttons → the .extraMesButtons template; top bar (#top-settings-holder) rarely. Reuse ST classes instead of inventing your own: menu_button, text_pole, checkbox_label, flex-container, marginBot10, Font Awesome 6 icons (fa-solid fa-*). Style with theme variables — var(--SmartThemeBodyColor), --SmartThemeBlurTintColor, --SmartThemeBorderColor, --SmartThemeQuoteColor — zero hardcoded colors, so every theme and dark mode works. Mobile is non-negotiable: public/css/mobile-styles.css (media query max-width: 1000px) reworks the whole layout. No fixed pixel widths (width: 100%, flex wrap), touch targets ≥ 2.5em, nothing hover-only (everything must work on tap), keep controls out of the top corners (covered by panels), prefer callGenericPopup over custom floating panels (popups are already responsive), long lists get max-height in vh + overflow-y: auto, and remember the keyboard eats about half the viewport. getContext().isMobile() exists, but prefer responsive CSS over JS branching.

18.content: A manifest generate_interceptor names a globalThis function called before every generation: globalThis.myInterceptor = async (chat, contextSize, abort, type) => { ... }. chat is a mutable COPY of the prompt-bound message list — edit, inject, or remove messages here to shape the prompt without touching the real chat. abort(true) cancels the generation immediately; abort(false) cancels after remaining interceptors run. Event-based alternatives: CHAT_COMPLETION_PROMPT_READY ({ chat, dryRun }) is the final messages array for chat-completion APIs — last chance to edit; GENERATE_AFTER_COMBINE_PROMPTS ({ prompt, dryRun }) is the text-completion equivalent; CHAT_COMPLETION_SETTINGS_READY exposes the request body to tweak sampler/model params per request. Always check dryRun and bail — dry runs are prompt-size probes, not real generations. For persistent injections prefer setExtensionPrompt(key, value, position, depth) from code or /inject from scripts; both survive without re-running logic each generation.

19.content: ST's frontend is event-driven around one emitter: const { eventSource, eventTypes } = SillyTavern.getContext(); eventSource.on/once/removeListener, makeFirst/makeLast to control order. emit() AWAITS all listeners in order — async handlers can mutate pipeline data, but slow handlers block generation. Key events: APP_READY (start extensions here; fire-once semantics — listeners attached after it fired still run), CHAT_CHANGED (chatId — re-read chatMetadata, reset per-chat state), MESSAGE_SENT / MESSAGE_RECEIVED (messageId — added to context.chat, before render), USER_MESSAGE_RENDERED / CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED (DOM exists — attach message-level UI), MESSAGE_EDITED / MESSAGE_DELETED / MESSAGE_SWIPED, MORE_MESSAGES_LOADED (lazy scrollback — re-decorate), GENERATION_STARTED (type, params, dryRun — BAIL if dryRun), CHAT_COMPLETION_PROMPT_READY, STREAM_TOKEN_RECEIVED (full text so far, every chunk), GENERATION_STOPPED / GENERATION_ENDED (cleanup), WORLD_INFO_ACTIVATED (entry list), GROUP_MEMBER_DRAFTED (per speaker in groups), PERSONA_CHANGED. Full catalog: public/scripts/events.js — always reference eventTypes.X, never string literals (a few legacy names are camelCase). Patterns: debounce bursty events (CHAT_CHANGED plus N RENDERED events fire together on chat load); set a re-entrancy flag if a MESSAGE_RECEIVED handler itself sends or generates, or it loops; message decoration must run in RENDERED events AND on CHAT_CHANGED / MORE_MESSAGES_LOADED, or it misses messages rendered before init.

20.content: The built-in Regex extension runs find/replace scripts over chat text and/or the outgoing prompt. RegexScriptData fields (typedef in public/scripts/char-data.js): id (UUID), scriptName, findRegex, replaceString, trimStrings[], placement[], disabled, markdownOnly, promptOnly, runOnEdit, substituteRegex (expand macros inside findRegex before matching), minDepth, maxDepth. placement values (public/scripts/extensions/regex/engine.js): 1 = user input, 2 = AI output, 3 = slash commands, 5 = World Info, 6 = reasoning blocks (0 and 4 are deprecated/legacy). The key lever is markdownOnly vs promptOnly: markdownOnly alters only what is DISPLAYED (the model still sees the original — cosmetic cleanup); promptOnly alters only what the MODEL sees (display untouched — strip bookkeeping, enforce formats upstream); neither flag = both. /regex-state reports whether a named script is enabled and where it applies. Cards can embed scripts in data.extensions.regex_scripts — users must approve them on import; use for formatting the card depends on (e.g., stripping stat blocks from display while keeping them in the prompt). Separately, World Info keys accept '/pattern/flags' regex strings in key arrays — the right tool for stems, plurals, and misspellings.

21.content: Reasoning is first-class: ST stores a message's reasoning in mes.extra.reasoning (with reasoning_duration and reasoning_type) and renders it as a collapsible block above the reply text; the block is click-to-edit like the message itself. Chat Completion APIs with native reasoning: enable 'Request model reasoning' and pick a reasoning effort where the API supports it — ST captures the reasoning automatically. Models that inline <think>...</think> in their output (common on Text Completion): configure Reasoning formatting in Advanced Formatting (prefix/suffix + auto-parse) so ST strips it into the reasoning block; /reasoning-parse converts inline tags in an existing message. Slash commands: /reasoning-get, /reasoning-set, /reasoning-parse, /reasoning-format, /reasoning-template, /reasoning-collapse, /reasoning-expand, /reasoning-toggle (collapse/expand/toggle accept message ranges). Regex scripts can rewrite reasoning text with placement 6. Reasoning from earlier messages is NOT sent back to the model unless 'Add to Prompts' is enabled (power_user.reasoning.add_to_prompts, with a max-additions cap) — remember that when a user asks why the model forgot its own reasoning.

22.comment: Debugging: where truth lives & common failures
22.content: Where the truth is: browser console (frontend errors + the World Info activation log; on mobile use chrome://inspect over USB or an Eruda bookmarklet), the server terminal (the ACTUAL upstream error body when generation fails; startup logs list every loaded/failed extension), prompt itemization — the 🧾 icon on any AI message shows the exact prompt sent with per-section token counts; if something isn't in the itemization it was never sent, which settles every 'why doesn't the model know X' question — the Network tab (all /api traffic; 403 = CSRF/whitelist, usually a missing getRequestHeaders()), and User Settings > Debug Menu. Frequent failures: changes vanish on reload = a debounced saver was never called; extension missing from the list = invalid manifest.json or a nested folder (repo/repo/manifest.json); listed but dead = JS error at import time (first console line naming the extension path — often a broken relative import; use getContext()); \{\{macro\}\} printed literally = that surface doesn't expand macros (typed chat messages don't); STscript stops mid-chain = one command errored — bisect with /echo \{\{pipe\}\} between stages; chat file 'corrupted' = a hand-edited jsonl line no longer parses (every line must parse independently). Techniques: reproduce on a clean profile with node server.js --dataRoot /tmp/st-test; SillyTavern.getContext() in the console is a live REPL (getContext().chat.at(-1), getContext().chatMetadata.variables); after an ST update, diff public/scripts/st-context.js for renamed exports.

23.content: Server config lives in config.yaml at the repo root; a server RESTART is required after edits. Access from a phone or another LAN device needs two things: listen: true (bind beyond localhost) AND the client allowed through the whitelist — either add the device's IP (CIDR ranges like 192.168.1.0/24 work) to the whitelist list, or set whitelistMode: false to disable it, in which case enable basicAuth (basicAuthMode: true plus basicAuthUser credentials) so the instance isn't open to everyone. Default port: 8000 (port key). enableExtensions toggles the extension system. Symptoms: connection refused = listen is false or a firewall; a 403 'Forbidden: Connection attempt from <IP>' in the server log = whitelist. Never expose an unauthenticated ST directly to the internet — use basicAuth, a VPN (Tailscale), or a reverse proxy with auth. Multi-user: user accounts each get their own data/<handle>/ tree; the default handle is default-user.

24.content: Characters on disk: data/<handle>/characters/<Name>.png — the card JSON is base64-encoded in a 'chara' PNG tEXt chunk (V3 also writes a 'ccv3' chunk). Never edit the PNG as an image; edit through ST or the endpoints. Bare JSON imports fine. Node-side chunk parser: src/character-card-parser.js. In the browser: context.characters[context.characterId] — card data under .data, avatar filename in .avatar (ST's stable character identifier). Chats: data/<handle>/chats/<Character Name>/*.jsonl — line 1 is metadata (user_name, character_name, chat_metadata including variables), each subsequent line one message object ({ name, is_user, mes, extra, swipes, ... }); every line must parse as standalone JSON. Worlds: data/<handle>/worlds/*.json. Server endpoints (JSON POST with getRequestHeaders() for CSRF): /api/characters/import, /api/characters/export, /api/characters/merge-attributes (partial card edit). World Info from code: const data = await ctx.loadWorldInfo('Name') → { entries: {...} } (cached clone); mutate; await ctx.saveWorldInfo('Name', data); then ctx.reloadWorldInfoEditor(name) if the editor is open. createWorldInfoEntry(null, data) (exported from public/scripts/world-info.js) appends an entry with correct defaults and uid. From scripts: /getentryfield, /setentryfield, /createentry file=X key=Y content, /findentry, /world name.

25.content: Tavernkeeper's Workshop (the extension the user runs) turns workshop function tools and tagged st-* fences into real SillyTavern objects; the fence protocol, current mode, and this knowledge index are injected into the prompt by the extension itself. Operation: plan mode (default) renders Apply/Dismiss cards under detected blocks and queues tool calls for approval; auto mode applies ordinary deliverables as they arrive. STscript, script-capable artifacts, and every managed-extension change always wait for explicit approval. /workshop-mode plan|auto|toggle reads or sets the mode, /workshop-apply [mesId] applies pending message deliverables, and /workshop-queue reviews queued function-tool calls. The optional Tavernkeeper Writer server companion adds workshop_list_extension_projects, workshop_get_extension_project, and workshop_get_extension_revision read tools plus workshop_create_extension, workshop_adopt_extension, workshop_patch_extension, and workshop_rollback_extension write tools. Managed folders use server-verified ownership metadata, optimistic revisions, server-side validation, atomic writes, retained snapshots, and a mandatory code-diff review. Always read a managed project immediately before patching it; stale revisions are rejected rather than merged. Other write tools cover characters, lorebooks, lorebook entries, Quick Reply sets, regex scripts, and STscript. Other read tools include workshop_list_lorebooks and workshop_get_lorebook, workshop_list_characters and workshop_get_character, workshop_list_qr_sets and workshop_get_qr_set, workshop_list_regex_scripts, and workshop_search_knowledge. Applied/dismissed state is stored per message keyed by content hash, so reloads, edits, and swipes re-offer only genuinely new content. Drafts that must NOT be applied belong in jsonc or text fences, never st-* tags; a deliverable whose JSON contains ``` lines goes in a longer (four-backtick) fence.
